data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-9e4c238d">
    <title>auto-routing: bare 'ddx work' rejects with 'no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected' — DDx pre-flight too strict; should pass through to fizeau</title>
    <description>
OBSERVED (operator report, 2026-05-03)
Bare `ddx work` (no --harness, no --model, no --provider, no --profile pins) repeatedly rejects with:
  "no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected"
No reason logged for any of the 4 rejections. Same machine has codex working fine via direct invocation; codex has been running all day on niflheim. The rejections happen BEFORE the request reaches fizeau, so fizeau never gets a chance to pick a harness.

EXPECTED
Bare `ddx work` should pass through to fizeau with NO routing pins; fizeau should pick any available harness with quota. DDx should NOT pre-reject candidates based on its own (likely stale or incomplete) view of provider availability.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES
1. cli/internal/agent/runner.go ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService (or its caller at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go around line 1647) is enforcing pre-flight provider availability checks against a stale or incorrect provider list.
2. cli/internal/escalation/InferTier or similar is computing a tier that has no candidate matches, treating that as fatal instead of passing through to fizeau's auto-routing.
3. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go is constructing ServiceExecuteRequest with constraints that exclude all 4 known harnesses despite operator passing no pins.
4. The 4 candidates being rejected may be the 4 harnesses (claude/codex/agent/embedded); reasons could include: stale RouteStatus from fizeau, missing capability declaration, provider-health classification, cost-cap pre-check.
5. Related to ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap with explicit harness pin) but distinct — that's about filling Profile/MinPower; this is about DDx rejecting before fizeau gets called at all.

INVESTIGATION REQUIRED
- Capture the actual rejection reasons for each of the 4 candidates (add structured logging or read existing route_attempt events from the bead's event stream).
- Compare DDx's view of harness availability vs fizeau's `svc.ListHarnesses` / `svc.RouteStatus`.
- Verify that bare `ddx work` (no pins, no opaque-passthrough) actually invokes fizeau's auto-routing and not DDx's local pre-filter.

FIX SHAPE
- DDx pre-flight should refuse to filter candidates that fizeau hasn't explicitly excluded. If DDx knows nothing pinned, the request goes through to fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"", Model:"", Profile:"<inferred-tier>"}` and fizeau picks.
- "No viable routing candidate" error should ONLY come from fizeau, with fizeau's reasons surfaced. DDx should not synthesize its own rejection.
- If DDx has any pre-filter today, log per-candidate reasons so operator can see why each was rejected.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fizeau-side routing improvements (separate bead ddx-1e516bc9 if needed)
- Reshaping the cobra flag surface
- Changing default tier inference

INTERSECTION
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): if the unification covers the per-attempt RouteRequest construction, this fix might naturally fold in.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap): if fizeau auto-routes within explicit harness, that fix complements this. They're distinct: this bead is about DDx not over-filtering BEFORE fizeau sees the request.
- ddx-848069a3 C8 (routing preflight to Drain startup): if the preflight relocates as part of the refactor, the fix should land in the new location.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Investigation report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/auto-routing-rejection.md identifies WHICH function in DDx's pre-flight rejects all 4 candidates and WHY. File:line citation for each rejection point.
2. Fix: DDx pre-flight no longer rejects candidates that fizeau has not explicitly excluded. Bare `ddx work` (no pins) passes a maximally-permissive ServiceExecuteRequest to fizeau (Harness="", Model="", Profile inferred from bead tier OR empty for fizeau to choose).
3. If DDx must reject any candidate, the rejection event includes structured per-candidate reason (e.g., "candidate=claude rejected: quota_exhausted via fizeau RouteStatus").
4. Manual verification on this repo: bare `ddx work` against current bead queue successfully claims and starts a bead with codex (or whichever harness fizeau picks). Capture command output + first 5 events of the resulting bead.
5. Test: TestAutoRoute_BareWork_NoPreflightRejection — fixture ServiceExecuteRequest with empty pins; assert it reaches fizeau without DDx pre-rejection.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
7. Existing tests asserting that DDx pre-validates harnesses (if any) are reviewed: keep if they validate operator-explicit pins; delete if they validate auto-routing (which fizeau owns).
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:routing, kind:fix, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="9f7d893bff9d4788459f259b6ff5e83c96ad430b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Investigation report and no_changes_rationale written. Bead premise is incorrect at base rev 9f7d893b — `ddx work` already passes maximally-permissive `Harness=""`, `Model=""` to fizeau, never calls `ResolveRoute` (enforced by both `routinglint_test.go` AST guard and the `TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute` / `TestWorkPassesEmptyHarnessToService` runtime tests, all green at this rev).

The "no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected" string is formatted in `fizeau@v0.10.4/internal/routing/engine.go:260`, inside fizeau's own routing engine, invoked from `Service.Execute`. The request *does* reach fizeau; fizeau is the rejector. Fix belongs upstream (recommend folding into ddx-1e516bc9 or filing a fizeau bead).

Evidence written to `.ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/auto-routing-rejection.md` (full file:line walk of each hypothesis) and `no_changes_rationale.txt` (with verification_command). No commit.
